# Software Factory for VS Code 2.6

Release 2.6 hardens the post-2.5 baseline for production-oriented runtime use.
The factory now has a bounded MCP runtime manager contract for lifecycle
operations, a stronger internal production gate with Docker build parity and
recovery proof, and a quota-governance stack that makes provider-aware limits,
fairness, and shared admission control explicit for real request traffic.

## Highlights

- **Internal production gate hardened** — blocking Docker build parity,
  promoted runtime proofs, supported backup/restore workflows, diagnostics, and
  day-two runbooks now form a clearer production-readiness story.
- **Runtime lifecycle contract tightened** — runtime manager work now gives
  `status`, `preflight`, verification, suspend/resume, repair, and cleanup
  flows one bounded truth surface.
- **Quota governance and fairness** — provider-aware policies, workspace-global
  coordination, concurrency leasing, requester-lineage fairness, and load
  validation now govern shared request budgets more explicitly.

## Included enhancements

- MCP runtime manager contract plus unified runtime snapshots for
  lifecycle-sensitive commands
- fail-closed internal production mode, promoted Docker E2E runtime proof, and
  supported backup/restore and disaster-recovery workflows
- machine-readable monitoring/diagnostics surfaces and incident-response/day-two
  operator runbooks
- provider-aware quota policy, limiter telemetry, hierarchical budget model,
  quota broker admission control, shared concurrency leases, and fairness
  feedback wiring
- diagnosable production parity groups (`docs-contract`, `docker-builds`, and
  `runtime-proofs`) while keeping the aggregate production gate authoritative
- architecture/reference cleanup around accepted ADR-014 guidance and ADR-007
  authority discovery

## Validation

Verified with:

- `./.venv/bin/python ./scripts/local_ci_parity.py --mode production` → passed three consecutive clean runs; the latest sign-off bundle at `.tmp/production-readiness/latest.json` reports `current_green_streak=3/3`, `final_signoff=ready`, and green `docs-contract`, `docker-builds`, and `runtime-proofs` groups.
- Production aggregate evidence: `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/` → `336 passed, 5 skipped`; `./tests/run-integration-test.sh` passed; required internal-production docs/runbooks were present; Docker build validation passed for all factory images; and the targeted Docker runtime proofs passed for strict tenant isolation, stop/cleanup restart behavior, and backup/restore roundtrip recovery.
- Post-commit release policy validation: `./.venv/bin/python ./scripts/verify_release_docs.py --repo-root . --base-rev HEAD^ --head-rev HEAD` passed for the `2.5` → `2.6` bump.
- Post-commit manifest parity: `./.venv/bin/python ./scripts/factory_release.py write-manifest --repo-root . --repo-url https://github.com/blecx/softwareFactoryVscode.git --check` passed on the committed release artifacts.

## Delivery status snapshot

| Scope                                            | Status    | Why it matters                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ------------------------------------------------ | --------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Practical per-workspace baseline                 | Improved  | The default operator path now has stronger lifecycle management, production-readiness proof, backup/restore support, and machine-readable diagnostics.                                      |
| Shared multi-tenant promotion (ADR-008 accepted) | Fulfilled | The 2.5 release already closed the promotion gate on `main`, and 2.6 keeps that claim honest while hardening the surrounding runtime and governance surfaces.                               |
| Whole implementation roadmap                     | Narrowed  | Internal production readiness, recovery proof, runtime lifecycle discipline, and quota governance advanced significantly, but future operational polish and optimization work still remain. |

## Notes for publishing

- Suggested tag: `v2.6`
- Canonical release marker: `VERSION`
- Machine-readable release source of truth: `manifests/release-manifest.json`
- Detailed release history: `CHANGELOG.md`
